issue_title: Keycloak wrongly assumes that the default datasource is the first one
issue_body:
### Area

dist/quarkus

### Describe the bug

Keycloak assumes, at least at the following places:
- [org.keycloak.quarkus.deployment.KeycloakProcessor, line 323](/keycloak/keycloak/blob/28fc5b4574ed5c8cc000ae11d5595d91af7f6a70/quarkus/deployment/src/main/java/org/keycloak/quarkus/deployment/KeycloakProcessor.java#L323)
- [org.keycloak.quarkus.deployment.LiquibaseProcessor, line  46](/keycloak/keycloak/blob/28fc5b4574ed5c8cc000ae11d5595d91af7f6a70/quarkus/deployment/src/main/java/org/keycloak/quarkus/deployment/LiquibaseProcessor.java#L46)

that the first datasource is the _default_ datasource.

While this assumption is correct for the `List<AggregatedDataSourceBuildTimeConfigBuildItem>` as produced by [`io.quarkus.agroal.deployment.AgroalProcessor.getAggregatedConfigBuildItems()`](/quarkusio/quarkus/blob/a2e7f7e8502b0e730083a301892e5a26251393c7/extensions/agroal/deployment/src/main/java/io/quarkus/agroal/deployment/AgroalProcessor.java#L279-L325), it is <ins>wrong</ins> for the `List<JdbcDataSourceBuildItem>` as produced by [`io.quarkus.agroal.deployment.AgroalProcessor.generateDataSourceBeans()`](/quarkusio/quarkus/blob/a2e7f7e8502b0e730083a301892e5a26251393c7/extensions/agroal/deployment/src/main/java/io/quarkus/agroal/deployment/AgroalProcessor.java#L229-L277). Notice that this method produces the datasources in the order determined by the iteration order of the `HashMap` returned by [`io.quarkus.agroal.deployment.AgroalProcessor.getDataSourceSupport()`](/quarkusio/quarkus/blob/a2e7f7e8502b0e730083a301892e5a26251393c7/extensions/agroal/deployment/src/main/java/io/quarkus/agroal/deployment/AgroalProcessor.java#L179-L193).

This wrong assumption leads to problems, such as those described in issue #14353, under the following circumstances:
- there is an additional datasource defined for Keycloak
- the database kind of the additional datasource is different from the database kind of the default datasource
- the name of the additional datasource happens to come before the name of the default datasource - `<default>` - as determined by the java's `HashMap` iteration order (an example of such a name is `test`; on the other hand, `atest` is OK, as it comes _after_ the `<default>`)

### Version

20.0.1

### Expected behavior

Defining an additional datasource has no negative impact in Keycloak.

### Actual behavior

Keycloak fails to start, reporting errors as described in issue #14353.

### How to Reproduce?

1. define an adittional datasource for Keycloak, for example in `application.properties`, as follows:
    ```properties
    quarkus.datasource.test.db-kind=oracle
    quarkus.datasource.test.jdbc.url=${DB_URL}
    quarkus.datasource.test.username=${DB_USER}
    quarkus.datasource.test.password=${DB_PW}
    ```
2. make sure that:
   * the DB kind of the datasource is different from the DB kind of the Keycloak's default datasource
   * the name of the datasource comes before `<default>` as determined by the java's `HashMap` iteration order (`test` does)
3. start Keycloak

### Anything else?

There is a workaround for this problem: Rename the aditional datasource to anything which comes _after_ the `<default>` as determined by the java's `HashMap` (or `HashSet`) iteration order - for example `atest`.
commit_message:
Keycloak wrongly assumes that the default datasource is the first one

Closes #15608

issue_title: Client registration service must not check client protocol for Bearer token
issue_body:
### Description

According to Keycloak documentation for [5,1,1 Bearer token](https://www.keycloak.org/docs/latest/securing_apps/index.html#authentication), bearer token can used for Client Registration.

However, SAML Clients can not be created with bearer token because Keycloak check for protocol in azp claim of bearer token. It is not logic. SAML Client must be registered if user has at least one of create-client or manage-client admin roles.

### Discussion

_No response_

### Motivation

Being able to create SAML Clients with Client registration.

### Details

_No response_

issue_title: Fix update of group mappers on certain changes of the group path
issue_body:
### Area

identity-brokering

### Describe the bug

In context of https://github.com/keycloak/keycloak/pull/13394, I have implemented an update mechanism for group mappers (currently there is only "Advanced Claim to Group"). This mechanism should update a mapper's group reference, whenever a group's path changes (due to group rename or move).

However, the mechanism does work in some cases, as further described below.

### Version

20.0.1

### Expected behavior

The mapper's group reference should be updated in the following cases (amongst other already implemented cases):

1. group rename: when an ancestor group is renamed
2. group move: when a group is converted from subgroup to top-level or when a subgroup's parent is changed

### Actual behavior

For case 1 mentioned above, the group reference is NOT updated at all.
For case 2, when using MapRealmProvider/MapGroupProvider, the group reference is updated as expected. For JpaRealmProvider, the group reference is NOT updated.

### How to Reproduce?

See new tests in the linked PR.

### Anything else?

_No response_
commit_message:
Fix update of group mappers on certain changes of the group path

The group reference in the mapper was not updated in the following cases:
- group rename: when an ancestor group was renamed
- (only for JpaRealmProvider, NOT for MapRealmProvider/MapGroupProvider) group move: when a group was converted from subgroup to top-level or when a subgroup's parent was changed

Closes #15614

issue_title: UserInfo: Role name mapper is not respected for user info endpoint
issue_body: ### Area core ### Describe the bug When enabling the "Add to userinfo" in the realm roles mapper like this The realm roles are added to the user info endpoint as expected. We then configured a "Role Name Mapper" for certain roles to rename them (we need this for software integration, where certain role names are expected). We did this like this ### Version 9.x.x ### Expected behavior We should expect that we have a role named "new_role" in the response of the user info endpoint. ### Actual behavior We have the original role named "old_role". For access token and id token it works as expected but not for the user info endpoint. ### How to Reproduce? See description. ### Anything else? I have tested this bug in version 18.x.x with the same result. Even when the OIDC spec doesn't define how to represent roles and groups specifically it states that claims have to be returned. This implies that a claim in a token like (realm_access.roles) should be represented exactly the same in the user info endpoint, I would guess. https://openid.net/specs/openid-connect-core-1_0.html#UserInfo
commit_message:
Implement UserInfoTokenMapper in HardcodedRole and RoleNameMapper mappers
Closes https://github.com/keycloak/keycloak/issues/15624

issue_title: oidc - JavaScript-Adapter LocalStorage#clearExpired does not clear all possible items
issue_body:
### Area

adapter/javascript

### Describe the bug

It does not really cause a huge problem but the code in `LocalStorage#clearExpired` looks suspicous because if keys who start with 'kc-callback-' are next to each other only every 2nd will be cleared.

https://github.com/keycloak/keycloak/blob/28fc5b4574ed5c8cc000ae11d5595d91af7f6a70/adapters/oidc/js/src/keycloak.js#L1622-L1640

If you look at the code you see it walks the local-storage and removes if the key starts with 'kc-callback-' but as `i` is advanced everytime and hence always the key exactly after the removed is missed. 

It is not a big problem because it will be simply cleared on the next call.

I have no reproducer for `keycloak.js` but the effect can be seen in browser dev-tools if you execute the code below:

```javascript
localStorage.length // prints 0
localStorage.setItem('kc-callback-a', 'a'); 
localStorage.setItem('kc-callback-b', 'b'); 
for( let i = 0; i < localStorage.length; i++ ) { if( localStorage.key(i).startsWith('kc-callback-') ) { localStorage.removeItem(localStorage.key(i)) } } 
localStorage.length  // prints 1 but it would be expected to be 0
```

### Version

main

### Expected behavior

I think the expected behavior is that the cache is clearExpired clears all possible entries and does not leave entries who could have been removed.

### Actual behavior

Not all possible entries are removed.

### How to Reproduce?

see bug description

### Anything else?

The simplest possible solution is to reverse the loop

```javascript
for (var i = 0; i < localStorage.length; i++)  {
```
to
```javascript
for (var i = localStorage.length - 1; i >= 0; i--)  {
```
commit_message:
Clear all entries stored in local storage (#34056)

Closes #15635

Signed-off-by: Jon Koops <jonkoops@gmail.com>

commit_message:
Use LOB handling query to select clients on Oracle

Closes #15639

issue_title: Internal pipelines don't tests run for OracleDB or other database
issue_body:
### Area

storage

### Describe the bug

The internal pipelines have been switch to run Quarkus. Apparently the fix in #13642 hasn't been incomplete, and when the tests run on `db-allocator` the `keycloak.storage.connections.vendor` is not set, so the DB settings forwarded to the Quarkus CLI.

### Version

20.0.1

### Expected behavior

Tests should run on Oracle and all other databases.

### Actual behavior

Tests run on H2 all the time.

### How to Reproduce?

Have a look at the CLIs created in the internal pipelines.
commit_message:
Fix query to work on OracleDB CLOB

Closes #15528

issue_title: Review `set-quarkus-version.sh`
issue_body:
We should review/reconsider the following:
* Use of `mvn versions:revert`.
   * This is sometimes causing issues like e.g. [re-introducing previously removed properties](https://github.com/keycloak/keycloak/pull/15601).
* Updates of dependencies only in `quarkus` module.
   * We should be probably also updating the dependencies in the root POM.
commit_message:
Use git checkout to rever changes

Closes #15644

issue_title: No localization support for consent label 'Has access to' on account v2 applications page
issue_body:
### Area

account/ui

### Describe the bug

The account ApplicationsPage.tsx contains texts that are not wrapped in a Msg.localize operation. It is therefore impossible to have them translated into different languages.

e.g.  <DescriptionListTerm>Has access to</DescriptionListTerm>
Should be
<DescriptionListTerm>{Msg.localize('applicationDetailsConsent')}</DescriptionListTerm>

Key to be decided

### Version

18.0.2

### Expected behavior

Localization support should be available for ALL visible texts on the account pages.

### Actual behavior

Some texts remain in English, regardless the support for localization.

### How to Reproduce?

Set the language to something else then english and you'll see some texts will always remain in english as there is no key to be set.

### Anything else?

_No response_
commit_message: Added label for "Has access to" (#19061)

issue_title: Password change sometimes triggers error
issue_body:
### Area

user-profile

### Describe the bug

When a user goes to Account Security > Sign in > Password > Update and enters a new password (+ confirmation) in about 20% of the cases an unhandled exception occurs. Interestingly, the password is changed. The error seems to have no relevance for the password update process. 

However, the user does not get an e-mail notification about the changed password. The fourth line of the trace may indicate that the error occured while sending the mail? The local Postfix does not get any incoming connection from the Keycloak application when the error occurs.

20.0.{1,2,3}

The error should be handled more gracefully, or the stack trace should be more helpful.

issue_title: clientID are base64 encoded in state uri param with non URI friendly base64
issue_body:
### Area

identity-brokering

### Describe the bug

Hi,

Since #10227 some clientID (in "state") are Base64 encoded but not in URI friendly form despite the warning in the pull request.

New code in IdentityBrokerState.

This new code use java.util.Base64 and can produce non URL safe parameter.
It produce url paramater "state" with + or /.
Sample : c5ac1ea7-6c28-4be1-b7cd-d63a1ba57f78 >  xawep2woS+G3zdY6G6V/eA

The "+" seems well encoded in url with %2B but some js adapter decode the complete uri and remplace %2B with "+".

"+" in URI is a "space".

The callback url is then like this with "+" instead of "%2B"

https://{host}/auth/realms/{realmName}/broker/{broker-name}/endpoint?state=EeW2DGPPk6sdZG6jgymATsRqZeNpj3iy6lP1QAMATnc.uJpIOdQ89-w.xawep2woS+G3zdY6G6V%2FeA&session_state={session}&code={code}

When state is decoded, the "+" is interpreted as "space" and the session is not found.

DEBUG [org.keycloak.protocol.RestartLoginCookie] (executor-thread-3) Skip restarting from the KC_RESTART. Clients doesn't match: Cookie client: cityzen.app.crm_frontend, Requested client: xawep2woS G3zdY6G6V/eA

In the original PR #10231 @ahus1 as already warn about : 
> The alphabet used by Base64 is not URL safe

@mposolda say 
>We have Base64Url class inside the codebase, which effectively does this. So hopefully using that class should be ok
..
>Please use org.keycloak.common.util.Base64Url to encode/decode to avoid reinventing the wheel for convert into URL friendly format.

But the code merged use :
(java.util.)Base64.getEncoder()
(java.util.)Base64.getDecoder()

The original PR was about to reduce the length of RelayState.
Using BASE64Url don't increase length

https://en.wikipedia.org/wiki/Base64#URL_applications
>using [URL encoders/decoders](https://en.wikipedia.org/wiki/Percent-encoding) is no longer necessary and **has no effect on the length of the encoded value**

I propose to use standard java.util.Base64.getUrlDecoder() and Base64.getUrlEncoder() instead or the internal
org.keycloak.common.util.Base64Url

Thanks

### Version

20.0.1

### Expected behavior

Don't encode clientID with "+" ou "/" in URI

Encode c5ac1ea7-6c28-4be1-b7cd-d63a1ba57f78 as Base64URI Friendly : "xawep2woS-G3zdY6G6V_eA"

### Actual behavior

c5ac1ea7-6c28-4be1-b7cd-d63a1ba57f78 is encoded as "xawep2woS+G3zdY6G6V/eA" in URI




### How to Reproduce?

Uuid wich is encoded with "+"  : c5ac1ea7-6c28-4be1-b7cd-d63a1ba57f78


### Anything else?

#10231
#10227
commit_message:
Use org.keycloak.common.util.Base64Url to encode/decode clientID

    fix #15734
    related #10227 #10231

issue_title: "Failed to add user 'admin' ..." should not be an ERROR
issue_body:
### Area

dist/quarkus

### Describe the bug

When using the environment variables `KEYCLOAK_ADMIN` and `KEYCLOAK_ADMIN_PASSWORD` keycloak logs this on startup:

```
ERROR [org.keycloak.services] (main) KC-SERVICES0010: Failed to add user 'admin' to realm 'master': user with username exists
```

In other words: This error always appears except for the very first startup of keycloak. When deploying to kubernetes, there is no way to set these environment variables only for the first deployment.

We forward all `ERROR` logs to our 24/7, so this is annoying. I fail to see why this expected situation should be logged as an "error".

### Version

20.0.1

### Expected behavior

Should be logged as an INFO.

### Actual behavior

Log-Level is ERROR.

### How to Reproduce?

Start a keycloak-container that uses the environment variables `KEYCLOAK_ADMIN` and `KEYCLOAK_ADMIN_PASSWORD` while using a persistent data storage. Then stop and restart the container.

### Anything else?

_No response_

KC_PASS=admin

for i in {1..30}; do
LOGINS="$LOGINS
(curl -s --fail --show-error "${SERVER_URL}/realms/master/protocol/openid-connect/token" \
-H 'Content-Type: application/x-www-form-urlencoded' \
-d "client_id=admin-cli" \
-d "username=${KC_USER}" \
-d "password=${KC_PASS}" \
-d 'grant_type=password' ) &"
done
eval $LOGINS
exit 0
```

issue_title: Realm localization: Inconsistent message resolving regarding language fallbacks for different themes
issue_body:
### Area

core

### Describe the bug

Currently two different approaches are taken regarding language fallbacks, when realm localization texts are specified.
This is difficult to understand for the users and should be unified.

Furthermore, in contrast to the message resolving of the properties files (see https://github.com/keycloak/keycloak/blob/fc7c57ee12c61730bad089b190b5e2e51c24d3e2/services/src/main/java/org/keycloak/theme/DefaultThemeManager.java#L224), none of these approaches currently considers the fallbacks for variants (e.g. "de-CH-1996" should have fallback "de-CH") and regions (e.g. "de-CH" should have fallback "de").

Currently there are the following approaches.
To keep the text short, I will use the abbreviations **S** (for **S**tatic i18n aka "theme properties") and **RL** (for **R**ealm **L**ocalization).

### Approach 1: "Realm-Localization-Default-Text with lower prio than static i18n"
The prio of messages is the following (highest prio first): **RL \<complete-language-tag>, S \<language>-\<region>-\<variant>, S \<language-region>, S \<language>, S \<default-language>, RL \<default-language>**

This approach is currently used in the following contexts (but doesn't work currently due to https://github.com/keycloak/keycloak/issues/15059):
- login-templates (FreeMarkerLoginFormsProvider)
- email-templates (FreeMarkerEmailTemplateProvider)
- account templates (FreeMarkerAccountProvider)

It is pretty clear that this approach is not very helpful: The purpose of realm localization should be to customize localization, and this is not possible when _RL \<default-language>_ has a lower prio than _S \<default-language>_.
One example: In message_en.properties for our custom login theme, we have a default value of true for key theme.hideLocalizationDropdown. (We decide based on this value whether to hide the localization dropdown.) One of our customers changes this to false, via realm localization text for the default language en. (She wants that all users see the dropdown, regardless of their language.) Then users with language de will still see no dropdown, due to theme.hideLocalizationDropdown still being true, because _S \<default-language>_ has a higher priority than _RL \<default-language>_.

### Current Approach 2: "Realm-Localization-Default-Text with higher prio than static i18n"
The prio of messages is the following (highest prio first): **RL \<complete-language-tag>, RL \<default-language>, S \<language>-\<region>-\<variant>, S \<language-region>, S \<language>, S \<default-language>**

This approach works fine for the example above (theme.hideLocalizationDropdown): It is possible to overwrite the static messages for all languages with the realm localization text of the default locale. But it is questionable whether this is the expected behavior in most cases. The following things should be considered:
- In custom themes, it is possible to overwrite messages from the base theme. For symmetry reasons, it makes sense that realm localization behaves the same as a custom theme, with regards to overwriting message texts. The prio for custom themes is as follows (highest prio first, **SC** = **S**tatic **C**ustom theme, **SB** = **S**tatic **B**ase theme): **SC \<language>-\<region>-\<variant>, SB \<language>-\<region>-\<variant>,  SC \<language-region>, SB \<language-region>, SC \<language>, SB \<language>, SC \<default-language>, SB \<default-language>**
- As a user, I would assume when I specify an English realm localization text for the Account UI title (there might be a typo in the Keycloak default text), this is NOT displayed in the German Account UI page - at least when everything is nicely translated to German. It might be confusing to have a completely German page, with an English title.

### Version

19.0.3

### Expected behavior

The priority of message resolving should be the same in all contexts. A common solution should be implemented that is
- well understood by users
- covers all common scenarios

We suggest to use the same approach that is used when overwriting messages in a sub theme.
The prio of messages would then be (highest prio first): **RL \<language>-\<region>-\<variant>, S \<language>-\<region>-\<variant>, RL \<language-region>, S \<language-region>, RL \<language>, S \<language>, RL \<default-language>, S \<default-language>**

### Actual behavior

Mentioned in description.

### How to Reproduce?

Mentioned in description.

### Anything else?

Solving this issue would also fix https://github.com/keycloak/keycloak/issues/15059.

We are happy to provide a PR for this - but wait for the maintainers' feedback regarding the suggested solution (see "Expected behavior")
commit_message:
Consistent message resolving regarding language fallbacks for all themes

- the prio of messages is now as follows for all themes (RL = realm localization, T = Theme i18n files): RL <variant> > T <variant> > RL <region> > T <region> > RL <language> > T <language> > RL en > T en
- centralize the message resolving logic in helper methods in LocaleUtil and use it for all themes, add unit tests in LocaleUtilTest
- add basic integration tests to check whether realm localization can be used in all supported contexts:
  - Account UI V2: org.keycloak.testsuite.ui.account2.InternationalizationTest
  - Login theme: LoginPageTest
  - Email theme: EmailTest
- deprecate the param useRealmDefaultLocaleFallback=true of endpoint /admin/realms/{realm}/localization/{locale}, because it does not resolve fallbacks as expected and is no longer used in admin-ui v2
- fix locale selection in DefaultLocaleSelectorProvider that a supported region (like "de-CH") will no longer selected instead of a supported language (like "de"), when just the language is requested, add corresponding unit tests
- improvements regarding message resolving in Admin UI V2:
  - add cypress test i18n_test.spec.ts, which checks the fallback implementation
  - log a warning instead of an error, when messages for some languages/namespaces cannot be loaded (the page will probably work with fallbacks in that case)

Closes #15845

storage

issue_title: Incorrect Signature algorithms presented by Client Authenticator
issue_body:
### Area

oidc

### Describe the bug

## Issue:

Presented signature algorithms for clients using client Client authenticator being "Signed JWT" or "Signed JWT with client Secret" are not correct

### Signed JWT client 

Doc states

```
Signed JWT is "Signed Json Web Token".

When choosing this credential type you will have to also generate a private key and certificate for the client in the tab Keys. The private key will be used to sign the JWT, while the certificate is used by the server to verify the signature.
```

Keycloak behaviour

1. Go to credentials
2. Select client authenticator "Signed Jwt"
3. Go to Signature algorithm

The proposed list of signature algorithm iss:
```
ES256
ES384
ES512
HS256
HS384
HS512
PS256
PS384
PS512
RS256
RS384
RS512
```

Client has his private key registered within a keystore. It means that asymmetric key algorithm are needs to be used.

The value HS256 HS384 HS512 are applying to HMAC which is a a symmetric algorithm. 

The client needs to use a asymmetric algorithm.

It is providing more confusion than anything else, and those values should be removed.

 

The list of proposed algorithms should be 
```
ES256
ES384
ES512
PS384
PS512
RS256
RS384
RS512
```

#### Signed JWT with client secret

The doc states

```
If you select this option, you can use a JWT signed by client secret instead of the private key.

The client secret will be used to sign the JWT by the client.
```

Keycloak behaviour:

1. Go to credentials
2. Select client authenticator "Signed Jwt with client credentials"
3. Go to Signature algorithm

The proposed list of signature algorithm proposes:
```
ES256
ES384
ES512
HS256
HS384
HS512
PS256
PS384
PS512
RS256
RS384
RS512
```

Here we are dealing with symmetric key encryption, where is no Keystore.

Even more, there is reference within the doc, or corresponding client authenticator panel to any JWKS URL.

Also the OIDC specification explicitly mention the HMAC based algorithm in the section for `client_secret_jwt` : https://openid.net/specs/openid-connect-core-1_0.html#ClientAuthentication

Therefore the proposed list should only contains the symmetric algorithm: 
```
HS256
HS384
HS512
```

### Version

20.0.0
commit_message:
Incorrect Signature algorithms presented by Client Authenticator
closes #15853

Co-authored-by: Jon Koops <jonkoops@gmail.com>

issue_title: CVE-2022-41946 - Information Exposure vulnerability in org.postgresql:postgresql
issue_body:
### Description

-   Package Manager: maven
-   Vulnerable module: org.postgresql:postgresql
-   Introduced through: org.keycloak:keycloak-quarkus-server-app@999-SNAPSHOT, org.keycloak:keycloak-quarkus-server@999-SNAPSHOT and others

### Detailed paths

-   _Introduced through_: org.keycloak:keycloak-quarkus-server-app@999-SNAPSHOT › org.keycloak:keycloak-quarkus-server@999-SNAPSHOT › io.quarkus:[quarkus-jdbc-postgresql@2.13.5.Final](mailto:quarkus-jdbc-postgresql@2.13.5.Final) › org.postgresql:postgresql@42.5.0

# Overview

[org.postgresql:postgresql](https://mvnrepository.com/artifact/org.postgresql/postgresql) is a Java JDBC 4.2 (JRE 8+) driver for PostgreSQL database.

Affected versions of this package are vulnerable to Information Exposure in the pgjdbc driver, which writes to the operating system's shared temp directory when the InputStream to either `PreparedStatement.setText(int, InputStream)` or `PreparedStatemet.setBytea(int, InputStream)` is larger than 2K. The temporary file is readable by other users. This is the default system behavior on Unix systems but not on MacOS.

**NOTE:** This vulnerability is only fixed for JDK 1.7. Systems using JDK 1.6 or below can work around the vulnerability by setting the environment variable `java.io.tmpdir` to a non-world-readable location.

# Remediation

Upgrade `org.postgresql:postgresql` to version 42.2.27, 42.3.8, 42.4.3, 42.5.1 or higher.

# References

-   [GitHub Commit](https://github.com/pgjdbc/pgjdbc/commit/34855cffb9079edf95cf77930f3e3b73f6a1b139)
-   [GitHub Commit](https://github.com/pgjdbc/pgjdbc/commit/9008dc9aade6dbfe4efafcd6872ebc55f4699cf5)

issue_title: Keycloak Export only accept H2 datase-URL (Datasource: URL format error; must be jdbc:h2 ... but is jdbc:mariadb: ...)
issue_body:
### Before reporting an issue

- [X] I have searched existing issues
- [X] I have reproduced the issue with the latest release

### Area

dist/quarkus

### Describe the bug

I like to **export Keycloak-data from a MariaDB database**, but it's not possible.

The source I use is: https://www.keycloak.org/server/importExport

The export command `export` **only accepts a H2 database-URL**, even I've created a build with proper database vendor. I've created a new build before execute the export: `build --db=mariadb`

There is an error displayed if I try to export the Keycloak-data from the MariaDB database. 

The error is:

`Datasource '<default>': URL format error; must be "jdbc:h2:{ {.|mem:}[name] | [file:]fileName | {tcp|ssl}:[//]server[:port][,server2[:port]]/name }[;key=value...]" but is "jdbc:mariadb://db.my-domain.de:3306/keycloak" `

### Version

20.0.1

### Expected behavior

The expected behavior is , that the `export` command accept a MariaDB database-URL like `jdbc:mariadb:...`.

### Actual behavior

The actual behavior is , that the `export` command only accept a H2 database-URL like `jdbc:h2:...` even if I execute `build --db=mariadb` before.

Also, the `show-config` commands shows that `kc.db =  mariadb (KcEnvVarConfigSource)` it isn't.

### How to Reproduce?

```
version: '3.9'

services:
  keycloak-export:
      container_name: keycloak-export
      image: quay.io/keycloak/keycloak:latest
      environment:
        KC_HOSTNAME: login.my-domain.com

issue_title: CVE-2022-42889 - Apache Commons Text prior to 1.10.0 allows RCE
issue_body:
### Description

# Description
* Package Manager: maven
* Vulnerable module: org.liquibase:liquibase-core
* Introduced through repackaging of commons-text transitive dependency as part of org.liquibase:liquibase-core

# Detailed paths
* `liquibase.version` defined as 4.16.1 in pom.xml

# Overview

Repackaged transitive dependency org.apache.commons:commons-text included by org.liquibase:liquibase-core is vulnerable to CVE-2022-42889.

> Apache Commons Text performs variable interpolation, allowing properties to be dynamically evaluated and expanded. The standard format for interpolation is "${prefix:name}", where "prefix" is used to locate an instance of org.apache.commons.text.lookup.StringLookup that performs the interpolation. Starting with version 1.5 and continuing through 1.9, the set of default Lookup instances included interpolators that could result in arbitrary code execution or contact with remote servers. These lookups are: - "script" - execute expressions using the JVM script execution engine (javax.script) - "dns" - resolve dns records - "url" - load values from urls, including from remote servers Applications using the interpolation defaults in the affected versions may be vulnerable to remote code execution or unintentional contact with remote servers if untrusted configuration values are used. 


# Remediation

Upgrade `org.liquibase:liquibase-core` to version 4.17.1 or higher.

# References

[NVD Entry](https://nvd.nist.gov/vuln/detail/CVE-2022-42889)
[Keycloak Defined Liquibase Version](https://github.com/keycloak/keycloak/blob/main/pom.xml#L135)
[Liquibase Release Including Fixed Dependency](https://github.com/liquibase/liquibase/releases/tag/v4.17.1)
[Liquibase Commit Addressing Dependency](https://github.com/liquibase/liquibase/commit/47a17396ee5e3382dfbfe7e8930887f52e0541a5)
commit_message:
Exclude commons-text from liquibase-core dependency

Closes #15915

issue_title: API call to get user profile config should allow any admin role.
issue_body:
### Before reporting an issue

- [X] I have searched existing issues
- [X] I have reproduced the issue with the latest release

### Area

admin/api

### Describe the bug

The REST call to GET the user profile config, `admin/realms/my-realm/users/profile` currently requires the `view-realm` role.  This causes a bug if a user has, for instance, `query-users` role combined with fine grained access.  The user profile config is used by the admin UI to dynamically create the user form in the admin console.  (It will soon also be used in account console)

There is no security problem with a user reading the user profile config as it does not provide any user data.  Therefore, the profile config should have `auth.requireAnyAdminRole()` instead of `auth.realm().reqireViewRealm()`.


### Version

main

### Expected behavior

UI can acquire the user profile config with any admin role. 

### Actual behavior

UI can only get user profile config with `view-realm` role.

### How to Reproduce?

See description above.

### Anything else?

_No response_

issue_title: CVE-2022-41854/CVE-2022-38752 Snakeyaml vulnerable to Stack overflow leading to denial of service
issue_body:
### Description

Overview

[org.yaml:snakeyaml](https://code.google.com/p/snakeyaml/source/browse/) is a YAML 1.1 parser and emitter for Java.

Affected versions ( < 1.32) of this package are vulnerable to Denial of Service (DoS) due to missing nested depth limitation for collections.

Keycloak is currently using Liquibase version 4.16.1, which is affected by the vunerability.

Details

Those using Snakeyaml to parse untrusted YAML files may be vulnerable to Denial of Service attacks (DOS). If the parser is running on user supplied input, an attacker may supply content that causes the parser to crash by stack overflow. This effect may support a denial of service attack.

Remediation

Upgrade org.yaml:snakeyaml to version 1.32 or higher.

https://github.com/keycloak/keycloak/issues/15089. In Keycloak the dependency is coming from org.liquibase:liquibase-core. This dependency has to be upgraded to version 4.17.0 or higher.

CVE-2022-41854/CVE-2022-38752 Snakeyaml vulnerable to Stack overflow leading to denial of service

Resolves #16062

issue_title: Title/header of Admin REST API page incorrectly shows placeholder
issue_body:
### Before reporting an issue - [X] I have searched existing issues - [X] I have reproduced the issue with the latest release ### Area docs ### Describe the bug As seen on https://www.keycloak.org/docs-api/20.0.2/rest-api/index.html: ```html ${product.name.full} Admin REST API
 

This is a REST API reference for the ${product.name.full} Admin REST API.

``` And showing in search engines: ### Version 20.0.2 ### Expected behavior The title/header should contain the actual product name. ### Actual behavior A placeholder for a variable is displayed. ### How to Reproduce? Go to https://www.keycloak.org/docs-api/20.0.2/rest-api/index.html. ### Anything else? This problem is very probably related to commit https://github.com/keycloak/keycloak/commit/97ae90de888362c82f6e87ef699035e573ca70ff and the changes in `pom.xml` in particular.
commit_message:
Fix REST API header showing product.name.full

Closes #16067

issue_title: kcadm do not update defaultGroups
issue_body:
### Describe the bug

Two groups were created "DEV" and "DEV1". I want to add them to default groups. Next Command do noting, defaultGroups are not updated:
"${HOME}/bin/kcadm.sh" update realms/bma -s  'defaultGroups=["/DEV","/DEV1"]'


Looks like it is old issue. Similar problem reported here also:
